title: AS-REP Roasting
briefing: The domain controller $TARGET logged Kerberos TGT requests (Event ID 4768) for account $USER1 with pre-authentication type 0 — meaning that account has 'do not require Kerberos preauthentication' set. An attacker can request its TGT and crack it offline: AS-REP roasting. Identify the exposed account and the requesting source.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.id: 100121. Look for data.win.eventdata.preAuthType: 0 on 4768 events. Read targetUserName + ipAddress.

questions.explain: 4768 TGT requests with preAuthType 0 = the account lacks Kerberos pre-auth, so its AS-REP can be roasted offline.

questions.prompt: Which field confirms the misconfiguration being abused?
questions.explain: preAuthType 0 means 'Kerberos pre-authentication not required' — the enabling misconfig for AS-REP roasting.
T1558.004 Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets: AS-REP Roasting.

options: Re-enable Kerberos pre-authentication on the account
Audit the domain for other preauth-disabled accounts
answer: Re-enable Kerberos pre-authentication on the account
Audit the domain for other preauth-disabled accounts
questions.explain: Turn pre-auth back on, rotate the (assumed-cracked) password, and sweep for every other DONT_REQ_PREAUTH account.
